ModMetadata.Name: Power Transmitter Plus
ModMetadata.Author: SixFive7
ModMetadata.Description:
[h1]Power Transmitter Plus[/h1]
Adds a visible beam, scrolling pulses, a configurable distance cost, new logic readouts, and wall and ceiling placement to the Microwave Power Transmitter.
Full multiplayer compatibility. Safe to remove from existing savegames.
[h2]Features[/h2]
[h3]Visible Laser Beam[/h3] A colored beam is drawn between a linked transmitter and receiver whenever the link is up, regardless of whether power is flowing. The beam is at full brightness at all times while visible, so you can see at a glance which dishes are actually connected. Power flow is shown by the pulse train (see below): when no power is flowing, the stripes freeze in place.
[h3]Scrolling Pulse Train[/h3] Energy pulses scroll along the beam from transmitter to receiver. Stripe spacing is constant in world meters (same look on a 5m link or a 500m link). Scroll speed scales with power throughput, so a lightly-loaded link is clearly distinguishable from a heavily-loaded one at a glance.
[h3]Distance Cost[/h3] Vanilla Stationeers caps microwave transmission capacity based on distance and hard-stops at 500m. This mod removes that cap and replaces it with a source-draw overhead: the transmitter pulls more from its source cable network per watt delivered, proportional to link distance. Formula: source pulls (1 + k * distance_km) watts per watt delivered. With the default k = 5: [list] [*]0 m: 1.0 watts in per watt out [*]100 m: 1.5 [*]500 m: 3.5 [*]1 km: 6.0 [*]5 km: 26.0 [/list]
[h3]Logic Readouts[/h3] Five read-only logic types are available on both the transmitter and the receiver, readable from configuration tablets and from IC10: [list] [*][b]MicrowaveSourceDraw[/b]: watts pulled from the source cable network [*][b]MicrowaveDestinationDraw[/b]: watts delivered to the receiver's cable network [*][b]MicrowaveTransmissionLoss[/b]: source minus destination (watts lost to distance) [*][b]MicrowaveEfficiency[/b]: delivered / source as a 0..1 ratio [*][b]MicrowaveLinkedPartner[/b]: ReferenceId of the linked partner dish (0 when unlinked) [/list] The power readouts return 0 when the link is down, the device is off, or no power is flowing. MicrowaveLinkedPartner returns 0 only when unlinked, regardless of power state.
[h3]Auto-Aim[/h3] A writable logic type [b]MicrowaveAutoAimTarget[/b] on both transmitter and receiver. Write a Thing's ReferenceId and the dish slews to point at it via the built-in servo; the base game's link raycast decides when the pairing actually forms, so obstacles in the path still take priority. Writing 0 disables. Manually adjusting Horizontal or Vertical cancels auto-aim. Cached targets persist across save/load and multiplayer join.
Two dishes auto-aimed at each other converge on a shared geometric fixed point regardless of mount orientation or distance. The post-load pass also re-solves cached pairs every save load and clears entries whose target has been deconstructed.
The host can disable auto-aim entirely via the [b]Enable Auto-Aim[/b] server setting. When off, MicrowaveAutoAimTarget is not registered at all: it does not appear in the tablet dropdown, IC10 compilation does not resolve the name, and writes to it no-op. Clients whose Enable Auto-Aim value does not match the host's are rejected at join time with a clear error.
[h3]Wall and Ceiling Placement[/h3] The transmitter and receiver dishes can be built on walls and ceilings as well as on the floor. The placement cursor cycles through every face the player aims at, and rotation hotkeys cover all three axes on non-floor faces. Any combination of mount surfaces can form a link, including floor-to-wall, wall-to-ceiling, and ceiling-to-ceiling, as long as the two dishes point at each other.
The host can revert to vanilla floor-only placement via the [b]Allow Non-Floor Placement[/b] server setting. Like Enable Auto-Aim it is restart-gated, and mismatched clients are rejected at join time.
[h3]Settings[/h3]
All features are configurable via the mod settings panel.
All settings are server-authoritative: the host's values control gameplay and visuals for everyone. Changes to the visual and distance-cost settings broadcast live to all connected clients on connect and on every change. Enable Auto-Aim and Allow Non-Floor Placement are restart-gated toggles, not live-broadcast settings: changing either requires a full Stationeers restart to take effect, and mismatches between client and host are caught at join time. The in-game settings panel groups the nine entries under five headers:
[b]Server - Features[/b]: [list] [*][b]Enable Auto-Aim[/b] (default true): Master toggle for MicrowaveAutoAimTarget. Requires a full game restart to take effect. Client and host values must match; mismatched joins are rejected [/list]
[b]Server - Placement[/b]: [list] [*][b]Allow Non-Floor Placement[/b] (default true): Build transmitter and receiver dishes on walls and ceilings as well as floors. Off restores vanilla floor-only. Requires a full game restart to take effect. Client and host values must match; mismatched joins are rejected [/list]
[b]Server - Distance[/b]: [list] [*][b]Cost Factor (k)[/b] (default 5.0): Per-kilometer overhead on transmitter source draw. k = 0 disables the overhead entirely; k = 10 doubles it compared to the default [/list]
[b]Server - Pulse[/b]: [list] [*][b]Stripe Wavelength[/b] (default 2.0): Distance in world meters between one pulse and the next [*][b]Scroll Speed[/b] (default 25.0): Pulse scroll speed in world meters per second at full power (5 kW delivered). Scales with sqrt(intensity) so low loads still move visibly [*][b]Trough Brightness[/b] (default 0.5): Beam brightness between pulses, 0..1 [/list]
[b]Server - Visual[/b]: [list] [*][b]Beam Color[/b] (default 000DFF): Hex RGB color. 000DFF is the vanilla cyan-blue [*][b]Beam Width[/b] (default 0.1): Thickness of the beam in world meters [*][b]Emission Intensity[/b] (default 10.0): HDR brightness multiplier on the beam color [/list]
[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[b]Requires:[/b] [list] [*][url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/]BepInEx[/url] [*][url=https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad]StationeersLaunchPad[/url] [/list]
[b]All players[/b] on a server must have Power Transmitter Plus installed. Matching mod versions are enforced during the connection handshake automatically.
[b]Dedicated servers[/b] need the same BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad + PowerTransmitterPlus setup installed server-side. The distance-cost simulation runs server-authoritatively and the handshake rejects mixed installs.
[b]Custom logic types[/b] registered by this mod sit in a reserved range outside the vanilla enum and the range used by Stationeers Logic Extended, so collisions with those are avoided. Another mod that registers a LogicType inside this mod's reserved range would collide; the exact range is documented in the source for mod authors who need it.
[h2]Reporting Issues[/h2] If you run into a bug or something behaves unexpectedly, please open an issue on [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/issues]GitHub[/url]. Please include the mod name in the title so reports can be triaged. Steam comment notifications don't always come through, so GitHub is the reliable way to make sure a report is seen.
[h2]Credits[/h2] [list] [*][b]ThunderDuck[/b]: Created [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3625190467]Stationeers Logic Extended[/url], which pioneered the pattern for registering custom LogicType values. The injection approach used here (extending ProgrammableChip.AllConstants, Logicable.LogicTypes, and the enum-name lookup paths) is adapted from that work. [/list]
[url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/tree/master/Mods/PowerTransmitterPlus]Source Code on GitHub[/url]
[h2]License[/h2] Licensed under the [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/blob/master/LICENSE]Apache License 2.0[/url]. Attribution required; see the LICENSE and NOTICE files at the repository root.
ModMetadata.ChangeLog: v1.7.2: Beam pulse position no longer jumps when power flow rate updates - Pulse stripes now integrate position incrementally each frame instead of multiplying total elapsed time by the current speed, so a speed change only affects future motion. Previously every speed change retroactively rescaled the stripes' world position, producing visible position jumps. Most noticeable with mods that update source-draw rates frequently, such as Re-Volt's proportional power sharing. - No multiplayer protocol change; v1.7.1 and v1.7.2 clients are wire-compatible.
ModMetadata.InGameDescription: Power Transmitter Plus Adds a visible beam, scrolling pulses, a configurable distance cost, new logic readouts, and wall and ceiling placement to the Microwave Power Transmitter. Full multiplayer compatibility. Safe to remove from existing savegames. Features: - Visible laser beam between linked transmitter and receiver dishes - Scrolling energy pulses, speed scales with power throughput - Replaces vanilla distance derate with a source-draw overhead (1 + k * distance_km watts drawn per watt delivered). Default k = 5. No more 500m hard cap. - Five read-only logic readouts (tablet + IC10): MicrowaveSourceDraw, MicrowaveDestinationDraw, MicrowaveTransmissionLoss, MicrowaveEfficiency, MicrowaveLinkedPartner - Writable MicrowaveAutoAimTarget: write a Thing's ReferenceId to auto-aim the dish; write 0 to disable. Host can disable the feature entirely via Enable Auto-Aim; join handshake rejects mismatched clients - Wall and ceiling placement for transmitter and receiver dishes. Host can revert to vanilla floor-only via Allow Non-Floor Placement; join handshake rejects mismatched clients Settings: Server-authoritative: the host's values control gameplay and visuals for all connected clients, broadcast live on change. Requires: BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad. All players, including dedicated servers, need the same setup.
